response.justification: Candidate is a senior/staff-level engineer with 13+ years of experience applying to an entry-level Junior Java Developer role — a severe seniority mismatch that creates high overqualification and retention risk. Java appears only as a minor skill with limited recent use; primary stack is TypeScript/Node/DLang. German is only Basic, which fails the role's requirement for strong German communication when working with German insurance clients. No insurance domain exposure.
strengths: Deep software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, agile, trunk-based development
Strong OOP fundamentals and broad multi-language experience including some Java
Based in Berlin, fluent English, comfortable with cloud/Docker/Linux
concerns: Massive overqualification for an entry-level role — high attrition and salary-expectation risk
German is only Basic, below the 'strong verbal and written German' requirement
Java is peripheral in recent work; primary expertise is TS/Node and DLang, not the JVM ecosystem

key_factors.factor: Seniority mismatch (junior role vs staff-level candidate)
Strong engineering fundamentals and OOP background
